description: Cross-rule coordination for resource lifecycle + data integrity anti-patterns (GCI0024 ↔ GCI0015). When resource leaks coincide with data corruption risks, compound risk triggers confidence boosts.

fixtures.name: Connection Pool Exhaustion + Over-Posting
fixtures.description: SqlConnection not disposed + mass field assignment from request. Result: Connection leak triggers pool exhaustion + attacker gains privilege escalation via over-posting.
fixtures.coordination_pattern: Resource leak + data injection = cascade failure
fixtures.compound_risk: Connection pool exhaustion compounded by privilege escalation attack

fixtures.name: File Handle Leak + Integer Overflow
fixtures.description: FileStream opened without disposal + unchecked cast on file offset. Result: Integer overflow causes wrong file position + handle leak causes OS resource exhaustion.
fixtures.coordination_pattern: Resource leak + bounds violation = data corruption + resource exhaustion
fixtures.compound_risk: File handle exhaustion prevents all subsequent I/O operations; corrupted offset reads wrong data

fixtures.name: Transaction Abort + Unchecked Cast
fixtures.description: ITransaction never disposed + unchecked cast corrupts WHERE clause. Result: Wrong customer's records modified + transaction locks held indefinitely.
fixtures.coordination_pattern: Resource (transaction) not disposed + data corruption in query = deadlock + data loss
fixtures.compound_risk: Cascading deadlock + data integrity violation affecting multiple customers

fixtures.name: Bulk Import Connection Leak + Mass Assignment
fixtures.description: SqlConnection created in loop and never disposed + three-field mass assignment. Result: N connections leak under load (N×[connection leak] + [privilege escalation]).
fixtures.coordination_pattern: Resource leak in loop + mass assignment = DoS + data injection
fixtures.compound_risk: Amplified leak (per-record) + attacker can inject fraudulent approved/creator fields

fixtures.name: DbContext Leak + Mass Assignment to Account
fixtures.description: DbContext never disposed + four-field mass assignment including IsEnterprise flag. Result: Context leak under concurrent load + attacker creates fraudulent enterprise accounts.
fixtures.coordination_pattern: Resource (context) leak + privilege escalation = infrastructure collapse + security breach
fixtures.compound_risk: Memory pressure + connection pool exhaustion triggered by fraudulent account creation

fixtures.name: SqlDataReader Not Disposed + Cast Bounds Violation
fixtures.description: SqlDataReader never disposed + unchecked cast on threshold parameter. Result: Reader holds connection open indefinitely + query returns wrong data subset due to overflow.
fixtures.coordination_pattern: Resource (reader) not disposed + query bounds violation = data corruption + connection leak
fixtures.compound_risk: Connection held open + wrong report data returned to user + repeated queries exhaust pool

coordination_rules.name: Resource Lifecycle + Data Integrity Coordination
coordination_rules.condition: When both GCI0024 and GCI0015 fire in same method or file scope
coordination_rules.rationale: Resource not disposed + data corruption = compound failure. Resource leak causes cascading failures when data is already corrupted. Connection/context/stream leak prevents cleanup paths.
coordination_rules.scope_detection: File-level heuristics: both findings in same method or same control flow block
coordination_rules.scope_trade_off: Conservative: method-level avoids false positives, may miss cross-method scenarios

known_challenges: Scope detection: Must determine if GCI0024 and GCI0015 operate on same resource
Heuristics trade-off: Variable name matching (fast, imperfect) vs control flow analysis (expensive)
False positive risk: File-level may be too broad, method-level may be too narrow
Implementation: Harder than P0/P1 due to semantic connection requirement
